title: 'Get out of here': Minister gets taste of 'akrosh' over traffic jam during BJP's Mumbai rally
content: During a BJP rally in Mumbai, a frustrated woman confronted Maharashtra minister Girish Mahajan and demonstrators, demanding they clear the road. She expressed anger over being stuck in traffic for over an hour while trying to pick up her child. The incident, captured in viral videos, saw the woman urging them to move off the road.

title: 'Don't need financial support, just prayers': Shapoor Zadran's fight for life in India
content: Former Afghanistan pacer Shapoor Zadran, who once stunned India’s openers Gautam Gambhir and Virender Sehwag in the 2012 T20 World Cup, is battling a rare, life-threatening disease called HLH in a New Delhi hospital. Supported by teammates like Rashid Khan, his condition remains serious, but his fighting spirit endures as he faces the toughest challenge of his life.

title: Two words that Apple's new CEO John Ternus used to describe his first day at Apple
content: Incoming Apple CEO John Ternus reflects on his 'exhilarating and intimidating' first day in 2001, recalling his work on the Cinema Display.  As he prepares to succeed Tim Cook, Ternus faces challenges in AI and mixed reality, despite strong iPhone sales and a hardware-centric leadership.

title: ‘Students to be expelled if they wed during academic year’: Bihar college diktat sparks row
content: A nursing school in Gopalganj sparked controversy with a notice barring female students from marrying during their academic session, threatening removal from rolls. The civil surgeon promptly cancelled the order, asserting education rights are not tied to marital status. An inquiry is underway against the principal, who claimed the notice was an advisory to prevent study disruptions.

title: 50 shades of anger? Bengaluru woman ties lover on pretext of ‘good time’, sets him ablaze
content: A woman in Bengaluru has been arrested for allegedly setting her boyfriend on fire during a BDSM session. The 27-year-old reportedly doused her partner in inflammable liquid and ignited it after tying him up. Investigators believe the act may have stemmed from resentment after he rejected her marriage proposal.

title: IPL: Kavya Maran, Abhishek Sharma’s father celebrate SRH star’s ton — watch
content: Opener Abhishek Sharma’s explosive 135* powered Sunrisers Hyderabad to a dominant 47-run win over Delhi Capitals, but it was the emotional scenes in the stands that stole attention. Cameras captured Kavya Maran and his father celebrating wildly, as the opener’s 10-sixes knock became one of IPL 2026’s most memorable innings.

title: Never been more optimistic about Apple future: Tim Cook in memo to employees on his exit
content: Apple CEO Tim Cook announced his transition to executive chairman later this year, after a 15-year tenure. He expressed confidence in Apple's future and its core values, praising the team's dedication. John Ternus, SVP of hardware engineering, will succeed Cook as CEO, with Cook pledging to support the smooth transition.

title: 'Bhooth Bangla' box office day 5: Akshay's horror comedy surpasses Rs 117 crore globally
content: 'Bhooth Bangla', featuring the versatile Akshay Kumar, has delighted audiences worldwide, surpassing the Rs 100 crore milestone by earning Rs 117.71 crore. While the film has sparked split opinions among fans, it experienced a robust start that set the stage for its continuing success, particularly with a notable boost in Day 5 earnings. Read the full article to know more in detail.

title: Meta to install tracking software on American employee's machines: Here is what the memo said
content: Meta is installing new tracking software on US employees' computers to capture mouse movements, clicks, and keystrokes for AI training. This initiative aims to improve AI agents' ability to perform work tasks autonomously, like using shortcuts. Meta assures employees the data won't be used for performance reviews, only for enhancing AI models.

title: 'Let bat do the work': Yuvraj Singh’s special message to protege Abhishek Sharma
content: Abhishek Sharma's unbeaten 135 powered Sunrisers Hyderabad to 242/2, securing a 47-run victory over Delhi Capitals. Sharma's career-best IPL knock, guided by mentor Yuvraj Singh's advice, showcased his free-flowing batting and the positive SRH environment. The win propelled SRH to third place in the IPL 2026 standings.

title: ‘What if I never come back?' A 3-year-old boy’s reply to his mother making internet cry
content: A viral Instagram video captures a poignant moment between a mother and her three-year-old son. When asked what he'd do if she left, the boy's sincere, tearful response about missing her and praying for her return deeply moved viewers, highlighting the profound bond and fear of loss.

title: ‘Show my dad on camera, see his reactions’: Abhishek Sharma’s sweet request wins hearts
content: Abhishek Sharma's unbeaten 135 powered Sunrisers Hyderabad to a commanding IPL 2026 victory over Delhi Capitals.  Post-match, he emotionally dedicated his performance to his father, who has guided him since childhood. Sharma also highlighted the team's supportive environment and his desire to entertain the fans with his aggressive batting.

title: Delhi to reel under heatwave from today; IMD issues yellow alert
content: Delhi is bracing for a heatwave from Wednesday to Friday, with temperatures expected to soar to around 44 degrees Celsius in some areas. A yellow alert has been issued as mercury levels are predicted to rise by 2-3 degrees Celsius over the next five days. The minimum temperature is also forecast to increase significantly.

title: ‘They will never be forgotten’: PM Modi pays tribute to Pahalgam victims
content: India marked the first anniversary of the horrific Pahalgam terror attack with Prime Minister Modi and the Army vowing unwavering resolve. The nation remembers the 26 innocent lives lost, with a strong message that terror will not succeed. India's decisive response, 'Operation Sindoor,' and subsequent 'Operation Mahadev' neutralized perpetrators, ensuring justice.

title: Oil prices today: Crude slips as markets weigh US-Iran talks, ceasefire extension
content: Oil prices dipped Wednesday after an initial Asian surge, as traders weighed US-Iran peace talk uncertainties following Washington's ceasefire extension.  Disruptions in the Strait of Hormuz continue to fuel market anxiety.  Geopolitical developments, including potential pipeline issues and upcoming US inventory data, are also influencing price movements, suggesting continued volatility.

title: ‘No deal if Hormuz reopens’: Trump extends ceasefire, claims Iran losing $500 million a day
content: President Trump extended a ceasefire with Iran, maintaining a blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. He stated Iran is suffering significant financial losses and must present a unified proposal for talks to advance. This decision followed a request from Pakistan's military chief and Prime Minister.

title: 'Trust & confidence reposed': Pak PM thanks Trump for ceasefire extension
content: Pakistan's Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif thanked US President Trump for extending the Iran conflict ceasefire, hoping for lasting peace.  Sharif expressed optimism for a 'Peace Deal' in upcoming Islamabad talks, emphasizing Pakistan's continued mediation.  Despite the truce extension, uncertainty lingers due to Iran's conditional engagement and the US naval blockade, though global leaders cautiously welcome the de-escalation.

title: Jude Bellingham invests in cricket, buys stake in The Hundred's Birmingham Phoenix
content: England and Real Madrid star Jude Bellingham is set to acquire a stake in The Hundred's Birmingham Phoenix franchise. The 22-year-old midfielder's investment, reportedly around 1.2% worth approximately £1 million, signifies a unique crossover between football and cricket. Bellingham, a childhood cricket enthusiast from the Midlands, is expected to formally announce the move this week.

title: Can listening to music while eating make food taste better?
content: Science reveals sound significantly impacts our food experience, influencing taste perception and enjoyment. Researchers found high-pitched sounds enhance sweetness, while low tones amplify bitterness. Tempo affects eating speed, and volume can alter food choices. This crossmodal connection, where the brain links sound with flavour, is already shaping restaurant experiences.

title: NFL trade: Dallas Cowboys and Kansas City Chiefs linked in unexpected $6M linebacker deal
content: Kansas City Chiefs and Dallas Cowboys are linked in a trade proposal involving George Pickens and first-round picks. The deal could boost Kansas City’s offense while helping Dallas move up in the draft. However, concerns over salary cap limits and the Cowboys’ defensive needs make the move uncertain as draft strategies take shape.

title: 2 days after Tamil Nadu, 13 die in Kerala cracker unit blast
content: A devastating explosion at a fireworks unit in Thrissur, Kerala, claimed at least 13 lives and injured many, with five in critical condition. The blast, occurring during preparations for a festival, destroyed four sheds. Intense heat may have contributed to the tragedy, which follows a similar incident in Tamil Nadu. An inquiry has been ordered.

title: IPL 2026 Orange Cap race: Abhishek Sharma surges past Klaasen to claim top spot
content: Abhishek Sharma has surged to the top of the IPL 2026 Orange Cap race with an unbeaten 135, now leading with 323 runs. Teammate Heinrich Klaasen is close behind with 320 runs, while Shubman Gill, Virat Kohli, and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi complete the top five. The competition remains intense with several players in excellent form.

title: Mike Vrabel divorce buzz grows as Jennifer stays silent amid Russini row
content: Mike Vrabel is facing growing scrutiny after his link-up with Dianna Russini sparked speculation about his marriage to Jennifer Vrabel. While no official statements confirm issues, commentators suggest possible personal consequences. The Patriots continue business as usual, and Russini has resigned, but public attention remains fixed on Vrabel’s personal life and rumored strain at home.

title: Dianna Russini’s troubles deepen amid Mike Vrabel affair claims
content: Mike Vrabel, the New England Patriots’ head coach, and the former New York Times’ NFL reporter, Dianna Russini, are still the talk of the town due to their alleged affair drama that sparked shockwaves across the league, a few days ago. While both have distanced themselves from the drama, it seems the scandal has now taken a turn for the worse.

title: London nightclub clash: Klaudia Zakrzewska hit by Gabrielle Carrington
content: Klaudia Zakrzewska, known as Klaudia Glam, is in critical condition after being hit by a car outside a London nightclub. Video shows a fight with Gabrielle Carrington before the crash. Carrington has been arrested, police confirmed. Two others were injured. Authorities are asking witnesses to come forward as the investigation continues.

title: MS Dhoni back at Wankhede! CSK legend drops massive return hint vs MI
content: MS Dhoni trained intensely for Chennai Super Kings. Will Jacks has arrived for Mumbai Indians. Both players are expected to feature in the Mumbai Indians versus Chennai Super Kings match. Australian pacer Spencer Johnson also joins the CSK squad. Injuries have impacted CSK's campaign. Ayush Mhatre is ruled out of IPL 2026.

title: IPL: Heinrich Klaasen creates history, joins Chris Gayle–Andre Russell elite club
content: Heinrich Klaasen continued his explosive run in IPL 2026, reaching a major milestone and joining an elite club alongside Chris Gayle and Andre Russell. The SRH star smashed a quickfire cameo against Delhi Capitals, capping a dominant batting display led by Abhishek Sharma’s stunning century.

title: Thrissur fireworks unit blast: Kerala CMO says three more bodies identified
content: The CMO said emergency workers have recovered several charred body parts from in and around the blast site. The district authorities will send them for DNA profiling to identify the victims. Two accident victims discharged after treatment

content: Atul Mathur is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India with over 27 years of experience in journalism. Based in Delhi, he has spent much of his career reporting on governance, public policy and politics, churning out researched, data-driven stories that impact daily lives. Atul is known for investigative depth and strong human-interest narratives as he strives to bring clarity and context to complex issues. He currently tracks the energy sector, writing on power, renewable energy, coal and mines.
